title: Yuho LLM Legal-Reasoning Benchmark Fixture
description: Each fixture pairs a natural-language scenario with the structural ground truth Yuho's evaluator computes from the same facts. Three tasks per fixture: section identification, element-set recall, and exception citation.

section.description: Canonical Penal Code section number this fixture exercises (e.g. `415`, `300`, `376AA`). Used for indexing; the LLM is asked to *predict* this in task T1.

scenario.description: Natural-language fact pattern presented to the LLM. Free prose. Should be neutral on the legal question and not name the answer.

section.description: Section number (or alphabet-suffixed variant) the scenario most directly fires. Computed by Yuho's evaluator + author confirmation.

satisfied_elements.description: Names of leaf elements satisfied by the scenario, derived from the encoded section's element graph.

fired_exception.description: Name of the defeating exception that prevents conviction, or null when no exception fires.

fact_facts.description: Boolean fact map fed to Yuho's StatuteEvaluator to compute the ground truth. Mirrors the `fact_facts` field in simulator/fixtures/.
